comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the functionality and pricing of teebot.dev, a service for printing text on shirts. Users express skepticism about the pricing, shipping limitations, and the technology used, while the creator explains the simplicity and purpose of the tool.

comments.author: TipsForCanoes
comments.text: Banner says "custom tees, delivered at cost" but then the preview page shows a price that is double what I get going to their drop shipper directly (printful). No detailed product info on shirt type or quality. No company info. Hard pass.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a detailed and critical observation about pricing discrepancies and lack of transparency, which are important factors for potential customers.

comments.author: theturtletalks
comments.text: What print on demand supplier are you using? I'm building a merch builder website for startups and most PODs (Gelato, Printify, Printful) all have their own issues.
comments.analysis: The comment asks a relevant question about the underlying supplier, showing industry knowledge and contributing constructively to the discussion.

comments.author: semiquaver
comments.text: Why does it take 20 seconds to composite a string onto a shirt? Are you using generative AI for something that can be done in milliseconds with traditional techniques?
comments.analysis: Raises a valid performance concern and questions the technology used, which is relevant for evaluating the service's efficiency.

comments.author: kleiba
comments.text: Btw, this has nothing to do with the "tee" shell command.
comments.analysis: Clarifies a potential misconception about the product name, contributing useful context.

comments.author: vzaliva
comments.text: I've tried to type in non-ASCII (Ukrainian) and preview shows it correctly. Does it guarantee it would print it properly?
comments.analysis: Raises a reasonable question about character encoding and print accuracy, which is important for international users.

comments.author: BaudouinVH
comments.text: I cannot click on "Preview and price" - I'm in a country you're currently not shipping to so it maybe a feature more than a bug.
comments.analysis: Provides useful feedback about shipping restrictions affecting usability.

comments.author: foxpress
comments.text:
i built teebot.dev because every time i wanted to put text on a shirt, i got lost in some enshitified print companies design flow. i just wanted to get words on a shirt.

So i made teebot.dev. type your text and ship it. that's it.

here's the CLI:

npx teebot "wtf?"
comments.analysis: The creator explains the motivation behind the product and shares a simple usage example, adding valuable context but with informal language.
